data.content:
## Handoff capsule — foolery-e750 SHIPPED

### Root cause
`wireTakeChildClose` in `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child-helpers.ts` and `wireClose`'s `logClose` in `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-io.ts` logged only `code=` (+ `signal=` for initial-io, but not for take-child-helpers) and `aborted=` on child close. The Node `close` event second arg (`signal`) was captured for the lifecycle event but dropped from the console.log in take-child. The runtime state's `exitReason` field ("timeout", "result_observed", "external_abort", "raw_close", "spawn_error") was NEVER read after being set — completely silent. A SIGTERM'd child that caught the signal and exited 0 was indistinguishable from a clean finish on the operator console.

### Fix summary
Added `lastStdoutAt: number | null` to `SessionRuntimeState` in `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` (lines 132-150, field list + jsdoc + null init in `createSessionRuntime` around lines 427-440). Set it to `Date.now()` on every stdout chunk in `doWireStdout` at line ~338. Created `src/lib/agent-session-close-diagnostics.ts` — a 63-line helper with `captureChildCloseDiagnostics(state)` (reads `exitReason` defaulting to `"normal"`, derives `msSinceLastStdout` and `lastEventType`) and `formatDiagnosticsForLog(diag, signal)` (emits ` signal=X exitReason=Y msSinceLastStdout=Z lastEventType=W` tokens that operators grep for).

- `terminal-manager-take-child-helpers.ts`: `wireTakeChildClose` captures diagnostics BEFORE `runtime.dispose()`, logs enriched line including `signal=`, and extends `recordTakeLoopLifecycle("child_close", ...)` payload with `exitReason`, `msSinceLastStdout`, `lastEventType`. `wireTakeChildError` gets the same treatment.
- `terminal-manager-initial-io.ts`: `wireClose` captures diagnostics before dispose, passes them to `logClose`, and enriches the `recordTakeLoopLifecycle("child_close", ...)` payload identically.
- `terminal-manager-take-lifecycle.ts`: `TakeLoopIterationTrace` gains `exitReason?`, `msSinceLastStdout?`, `lastEventType?` fields (lines 65-71); propagated through `logInteractionLifecycle` and `recordSessionFinishLifecycle`.

### Files touched
- `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` — `SessionRuntimeState.lastStdoutAt`, `createSessionRuntime` init, `doWireStdout` chunk handler (3 lines of change in function body).
- `src/lib/agent-session-close-diagnostics.ts` — NEW 63-line helper.
- `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child-helpers.ts` — `wireTakeChildClose` (lines ~172-215) and `wireTakeChildError` (lines ~217-255).
- `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-io.ts` — `wireClose` close handler (lines ~177-237) and `logClose` (lines ~236-262).
- `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-lifecycle.ts` — trace fields + both `logInteractionLifecycle` and `recordSessionFinishLifecycle` payload passthrough.

### Locking tests
- `src/lib/__tests__/agent-session-close-diagnostics.test.ts` — 9 unit tests: `lastStdoutAt` is null before stdout, set after first chunk, advances on subsequent chunks; `captureChildCloseDiagnostics` exitReason fallback to "normal", full field population, safe-on-null, handles non-string event type; `formatDiagnosticsForLog` emits the exact token strings (`signal=`, `exitReason=`, `msSinceLastStdout=`, `lastEventType=`) operators grep for, including `signal=null` / `msSinceLastStdout=null` edge cases. The format tokens are the regression lock: if a future refactor drops a field from the log line, these tests fail loudly.
- `src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-child-close-diagnostics.test.ts` — 2 integration tests that spawn a mock child, emit a stdout chunk, fire `close` with `"SIGTERM"`, and assert both (a) the `logLifecycle` `child_close` payload contains `childSignal="SIGTERM"`, plus string `exitReason`, number `msSinceLastStdout`, and has `lastEventType` property, and (b) the `console.log` child-close line includes `signal=SIGTERM`, `msSinceLastStdout=`, `exitReason=`, `lastEventType=` substrings.

### Why this will not regress
Two independent layers of tests: the formatter unit test locks the exact log tokens; the integration test runs the real take-loop path end-to-end and asserts both the console line and the lifecycle event payload. Deleting or renaming any of the four new fields — or regressing the log format — breaks at least two tests. The `captureChildCloseDiagnostics` helper centralizes the logic so all three close paths (take-child close, take-child spawn error, initial-io close) go through one function; no field can be dropped from only one handler without breaking the others via the shared helper.

### Reference commit hashes
- Fix: `910cb637` (was `31b70d6d` pre-rebase).
- Merge to main: `2c6b4626`.

### Branch artifact
- Pushed: `worktree-foolery-e750` → merged to `origin/main` → available at `2c6b4626`.

### Knot boundaries respected
- Did NOT add `watchdog_fired` lifecycle event (that is foolery-2782, already on main as `1a99ec1c`).
- Did NOT modify `terminateProcessGroup` (foolery-062f).
- Did NOT rename `onResult` to `onTurnEnded` (foolery-a401).
- Did NOT wire `onResult/onTurnEnded` in take-child (foolery-6881).

### Acceptance — all green
1. Child close log now reads: `code=N signal=X exitReason=Y msSinceLastStdout=Z lastEventType=W aborted=B iteration=I beat=BEAT` (take-child) / `code=N signal=X exitReason=Y msSinceLastStdout=Z lastEventType=W beat=BEAT` (initial-io take-loop). ✅
2. Signal comes from Node's `close` event second arg. ✅
3. exitReason read BEFORE `runtime.dispose()`; fallback to `"normal"`. ✅
4. `SessionRuntimeState.lastStdoutAt` exists and is updated on every stdout chunk in `doWireStdout`. ✅
5. `recordTakeLoopLifecycle("child_close", ...)` payload includes `childSignal`, `exitReason`, `msSinceLastStdout`, `lastEventType`. ✅
6. Initial-io `wireClose` mirrors the same fields. ✅
7. `bun run lint` / `bunx tsc --noEmit` / `bun run test` (251 test files, 2709 passing) / `bun run build` all green. ✅
